commands.review_summary:
The PR routes fal image generation success JSON parsing through `readProviderJsonResponse` and adds an oversized streaming-response regression test.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +51. Total +54 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main and `v2026.6.10` still call `await response.json()` on the fal image-generation success response. I did not run a local reproducer because this read-only review cannot execute tests or generate artifacts in the checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the fal 16 MiB success-body cap are the remaining merge gates; there is no narrow code repair queued.

target.title: fix(fal): bound image generation JSON response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`parseFalImageGenerationResponse` in `extensions/fal/image-generation-provider.ts` reads the image generation success response with an unbounded `await response.json()`. The shared fetch layer enforces an abort timeout but does **not** bound body size, so a misbehaving or compromised fal endpoint can stream an arbitrarily large (or `Content-Length`-less, never-ending) JSON body that `res.json()` buffers whole into memory — an OOM vector on the image generation path.

## Changes

- Route the success JSON read through the shared bounded reader `readProviderJsonResponse` (16 MiB cap = `PROVIDER_JSON_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES`, re-exported via `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http`): it reads under the shared 16 MiB cap, **cancels the underlying stream on overflow**, and throws `fal-image-generate: JSON response exceeds <N> bytes`.
- No new abstraction — reuses the same bounded reader as the recently merged provider response-limit PRs (#95218 / #96027 / #96607), bringing fal image generation in line with the other providers' bounded JSON reads.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: An unbounded fal image generation success response with no `Content-Length` and an oversized / never-ending body must not be buffered whole; the read must stop at the cap, cancel the stream, and throw a bounded error — while valid small responses still parse unchanged.
- **Real environment tested**: A `ReadableStream` Response delivering 32 MiB in 1 MiB chunks (double the 16 MiB cap) drives the real `provider.generateImage`. The test asserts the bounded reader cancels the stream mid-flight and never pulls the full advertised payload.
- **Observed outcome**: The provider throws `fal-image-generate: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes`. The underlying stream is canceled. `bytesPulled < 32 MiB` confirms no full buffering.
target.author: monkey-wusky
